title: Viewport performance: BVH raycasting, render-on-demand, wireframe optimization
analysis: Task addresses 4 viewport performance issues. (P-01) Raycasting in selection.ts:65-73 calls raycaster.intersectObjects on ALL meshes with no spatial indexing — fix by adding three-mesh-bvh for BVH acceleration on geometries and enabling firstHitOnly on the raycaster. (P-02) WireframeGeometry in selection.ts:168 is computationally expensive for large meshes, creating a line segment for every triangle edge — replace with EdgesGeometry which only computes edges where face normals differ significantly, a direct drop-in replacement. (P-03) Animation loop in Viewport.tsx:77-83 runs requestAnimationFrame continuously and calls renderer.render every frame even when completely idle — implement render-on-demand using OrbitControls 'change' event and a dirty flag, keeping the rAF loop alive (needed for damping) but skipping the expensive render call when nothing has changed. (P-04) meshManager.sync in meshManager.ts:116-117 creates new Set(Object.keys(meshes)) on every sync call — minor optimization to use direct property lookup instead. All existing tests use vi.mock('three', ...) with mock classes; new behavior can be tested by extending existing mock infrastructure. The controls module already exposes the OrbitControls instance so Viewport can listen to 'change' events directly without modifying controls.ts. Post-review: two robustness issues identified in meshManager.ts where computeBoundsTree() failures could leak GPU resources or leave BVH in inconsistent state.

prerequisites.description: Add three-mesh-bvh to gui/package.json dependencies and run npm install. This package provides computeBoundsTree/disposeBoundsTree for BufferGeometry BVH acceleration and acceleratedRaycast for Mesh.prototype.raycast. Required for P-01 (BVH raycasting).

analysis:
Analysis of the codebase reveals 7 CSS custom properties referenced in component CSS modules but never defined by applyTheme() in theme.ts: --reify-surface0 (FileBrowser, ExportDialog, Toast), --reify-surface1 (FileBrowser, ExportDialog, ReloadPrompt), --reify-surface2 (ExportDialog, ReloadPrompt), --reify-subtext (FileBrowser, ExportDialog, Toast), --reify-overlay0 (ReloadPrompt), --reify-green (Toast), --reify-red (Toast). These CSS files use fallback values (e.g., var(--reify-surface0, #313244)) so the UI renders correctly in dark mode, but theme switching won't affect these properties since they're never set as CSS custom properties by applyTheme().

The task description mentions --reify-font-mono as missing, but it IS defined via the 'fontMono' key in THEME_TOKENS and correctly converted by camelToKebab() to --reify-font-mono.

Additionally, applyTheme() is called inside onMount() in App.tsx (line 115), which runs after the initial render. This causes a potential flash of unstyled content (FOUC) since CSS variables aren't set until the component mounts. The fix is to call applyTheme() synchronously in index.tsx before render().

For T4 (spacing system), no spacing tokens exist — CSS files use hardcoded pixel values (4px, 6px, 8px, 12px, 16px, 20px). We'll define spacing scale tokens in THEME_TOKENS but defer CSS file migration to a future task.

Light mode (T2) is explicitly deferred per the task description.

Existing test file (gui/src/__tests__/theme.test.ts) has 4 tests: required keys check, Catppuccin Mocha values check, hex validation for color tokens, and applyTheme callable check. These will be extended.

REVIEW FIX: camelToKebab() only replaces uppercase letters, so 'space2xl' produces '--reify-space2xl' instead of '--reify-space-2xl'. This is a silent naming mismatch — CSS authors writing var(--reify-space-2xl) get the fallback value with no error. The fix extends camelToKebab to insert a hyphen at letter→digit boundaries when the digit sequence is followed by letters (e.g., space2xl → space-2xl). This preserves existing tokens like surface0/overlay0 where the trailing digit has no following letters.

steps.description: P-03 render-on-demand: Write failing tests in Viewport.test.tsx. Update controls mock to include addEventListener/removeEventListener on the controls object (currently just {}). Add test: 'animate loop does NOT call renderer.render when idle after initial frame' — use capturing rAF mock, fire first rAF callback (initial render), clear renderer.render mock, fire second rAF callback with no interactions, assert renderer.render was NOT called. This test fails against current code which always renders every frame.

P-03 render-on-demand: Implement in Viewport.tsx. Add let needsRender = true flag. Add requestRender() helper that sets needsRender = true. Register controls.controls.addEventListener('change', requestRender) to mark dirty on camera movement. In animate loop: keep calling controls.update() every frame (required for enableDamping), but guard renderer.render(scene, camera) behind if(needsRender), then reset needsRender = false. Add controls.controls.removeEventListener('change', requestRender) in onCleanup. Initial needsRender = true ensures first frame renders.

P-03 render triggers: Write tests verifying all re-render triggers. (1) 'controls change event triggers re-render on next frame' — fire controls mock's captured 'change' callback, then fire rAF, assert renderer.render called. (2) 'resize triggers re-render' — verify renderer.render is called on next frame after ResizeObserver fires. (3) Test that renderer.render is still called once for initial frame (existing test 'animate loop does not call renderer.render after cleanup/dispose' should continue to pass).

P-03 render triggers: Wire requestRender() calls in Viewport.tsx at all trigger points. Add requestRender() after meshManager.sync(props.meshes) in the mesh sync createEffect. Add requestRender() after selection.setHovered() and selection.setSelected() in their createEffects. Add requestRender() after resize() call in the ResizeObserver callback (alongside existing invalidateRect). Verify all tests from step-3 pass plus existing tests remain green.

P-02 EdgesGeometry: Write failing test in selection.test.ts. Add MockEdgesGeometry to the three mock (identical structure to existing MockWireframeGeometry). Add test: 'setSelected creates EdgesGeometry from mesh geometry' — verify that when setSelected('A') is called, EdgesGeometry constructor is invoked with mesh.geometry and the resulting LineSegments is added to scene. Update existing wireframe tests to use EdgesGeometry mock if needed. This test fails because current code imports/uses WireframeGeometry.

P-02 EdgesGeometry: In selection.ts, change import from 'WireframeGeometry' to 'EdgesGeometry' in the three import statement. In setSelected(), replace 'new WireframeGeometry(mesh.geometry)' with 'new EdgesGeometry(mesh.geometry)'. EdgesGeometry is a direct drop-in replacement that produces LineSegments-compatible geometry but only includes edges where adjacent face normals differ by > 1 degree threshold, making it dramatically lighter for dense meshes while preserving the visual outline.

P-01 BVH geometry: Write failing tests in meshManager.test.ts. Add computeBoundsTree = vi.fn() and disposeBoundsTree = vi.fn() to MockBufferGeometry. Mock the 'three-mesh-bvh' module to export computeBoundsTree, disposeBoundsTree, acceleratedRaycast. Add tests: (1) 'sync calls geometry.computeBoundsTree() on newly created mesh', (2) 'sync calls geometry.computeBoundsTree() after updating existing mesh geometry', (3) 'removeMesh calls geometry.disposeBoundsTree() before disposing geometry'. These fail because current meshManager doesn't invoke BVH methods.

P-01 BVH geometry: In meshManager.ts, import { computeBoundsTree, disposeBoundsTree } from 'three-mesh-bvh' and BufferGeometry from 'three'. Apply prototype patches at module scope: BufferGeometry.prototype.computeBoundsTree = computeBoundsTree; BufferGeometry.prototype.disposeBoundsTree = disposeBoundsTree. In createMeshFromData(), call geometry.computeBoundsTree() after all attributes are set. In updateMeshGeometry(), call geometry.computeBoundsTree() after updating all attributes (rebuilds BVH for changed geometry). In removeMesh(), call (mesh.geometry as any).disposeBoundsTree() before geometry.dispose().

P-01 BVH raycasting: Write failing test in selection.test.ts. Add mock for 'three-mesh-bvh' module. Add test: 'raycaster has firstHitOnly set to true' — after createSelection, inspect the mock Raycaster instance to verify firstHitOnly property is true. This test fails because current code doesn't set firstHitOnly on the raycaster.

P-01 BVH raycasting: In selection.ts, import { acceleratedRaycast } from 'three-mesh-bvh' and import Mesh from 'three'. Apply prototype patch at module scope: Mesh.prototype.raycast = acceleratedRaycast. After creating the Raycaster instance (line 42), set (raycaster as any).firstHitOnly = true. This enables BVH-accelerated raycasting that exits early on first hit, which is correct since we only use intersections[0].object.name.

P-04 sync optimization: Run existing meshManager tests to verify they all pass (behavior is unchanged, this is a pure refactor). Optionally add test: 'sync with empty incoming object removes all existing meshes' to ensure edge case coverage if not already covered.

P-04 sync optimization: Refactor sync() in meshManager.ts. Replace 'const incomingKeys = new Set(Object.keys(meshes))' and 'incomingKeys.has(key)' with direct property check: 'if (!(key in meshes))'. This avoids one Set + Array allocation per sync call while maintaining identical O(1) lookup semantics. Iterate meshMap.keys() for removal check, then Object.entries(meshes) for add/update (same as current).

Fix resource leak in 'resize triggers re-render on next frame' test (Viewport.test.tsx lines 273-302). The test overrides globalThis.ResizeObserver but restores it only at line 301 — not in a try/finally. If any assertion between render() and the restore throws (e.g. roCallback is undefined when the non-null assertion roCallback!() fires on a failed mount), the original ResizeObserver is never restored, causing cascading failures in all subsequent tests that depend on ResizeObserver. Fix: wrap the test body (lines 284-301) in a try/finally block so that `globalThis.ResizeObserver = OrigRO` always executes regardless of assertion failures. The finally block must contain only the restore; all assertions remain in the try block.

R-01 createMeshFromData resource leak: Write failing tests in meshManager.test.ts. (1) 'createMeshFromData disposes geometry and material if computeBoundsTree throws' — configure MockBufferGeometry.computeBoundsTree to throw an Error on next call (via mockImplementationOnce), call manager.sync({ A: meshData }), assert geometry.dispose() was called, assert material.dispose() was called, assert the mesh was NOT added to meshMap (getSceneMeshes().has('A') === false), and assert the mesh was NOT added to scene (mockSceneAdd not called for this mesh). (2) 'createMeshFromData logs error when computeBoundsTree throws' — spy on console.error, trigger same throw scenario, assert console.error was called with a message containing the entity path. These tests fail because current code has no try/catch around computeBoundsTree() in createMeshFromData.

R-01 createMeshFromData resource leak fix: In meshManager.ts createMeshFromData(), wrap the computeBoundsTree() call (line 66) and subsequent mesh creation (lines 68-70) in a try/catch. On catch: call geometry.dispose(), call material.dispose(), log console.error with the entity path and error, and return null. Update the return type to Mesh | null. In sync(), where createMeshFromData is called (line 140), check the return value for null before adding to meshMap and scene: `const mesh = createMeshFromData(entityPath, data); if (mesh) { meshMap.set(entityPath, mesh); scene.add(mesh); }`. This ensures GPU resources are cleaned up on BVH build failure and the mesh is never added to unrecoverable state.

R-02 updateMeshGeometry stale BVH: Write failing tests in meshManager.test.ts. (1) 'updateMeshGeometry removes mesh from scene and meshMap if computeBoundsTree throws on update' — sync mesh A, then configure its geometry.computeBoundsTree to throw on next call (mockImplementationOnce), sync A with updated vertices, assert the mesh was removed from scene (mockSceneRemove called), assert meshMap no longer has 'A' (getSceneMeshes().has('A') === false), and assert geometry.dispose() and material.dispose() were called (full cleanup, not leaving a zombie). (2) 'updateMeshGeometry logs error when computeBoundsTree throws on update' — spy on console.error, trigger same throw scenario, assert console.error was called with a message containing the entity path. These tests fail because current code has no error handling around computeBoundsTree() in updateMeshGeometry.

R-02 updateMeshGeometry stale BVH fix: In meshManager.ts updateMeshGeometry(), wrap the computeBoundsTree() call (line 114) in a try/catch. On catch: log console.error with the entity path and error, then call removeMesh(mesh.name) to fully dispose the mesh (geometry, material, BVH) and remove it from scene and meshMap. This ensures a mesh with updated render geometry but stale/missing BVH is never left in the scene where it would produce silently incorrect raycast results. The mesh will be cleanly re-created on the next sync() call since it won't be found in meshMap. Note: removeMesh needs to be called with the entity path, which is available as mesh.name (set in createMeshFromData). The function signature stays the same (void return) since the caller (sync) doesn't need to know — the mesh simply disappears from meshMap and will be re-added as new on next sync.

design_decisions.decision: Use EdgesGeometry instead of WireframeGeometry for selection overlay
design_decisions.rationale: EdgesGeometry only computes edges where adjacent face normals differ by a threshold (default 1 degree), producing far fewer line segments than WireframeGeometry which duplicates every triangle edge. This is a direct drop-in replacement that dramatically reduces compute time for dense meshes. A full stencil-based outline pass (OutlinePass + EffectComposer) would require setting up a multi-pass render pipeline — disproportionate complexity for v0.1.

design_decisions.decision: Use dirty-flag render-on-demand pattern while keeping the rAF loop alive
design_decisions.rationale: OrbitControls with enableDamping=true requires controls.update() to be called every frame for smooth deceleration. Stopping/restarting the rAF loop would cause damping to stutter. The dirty-flag pattern keeps the loop running (cheap controls.update() call) but skips the expensive renderer.render() when nothing has changed. OrbitControls' built-in 'change' event fires during damping, correctly triggering re-renders while the camera decelerates.

design_decisions.decision: Add three-mesh-bvh for BVH acceleration rather than custom spatial indexing
design_decisions.rationale: three-mesh-bvh is the de-facto standard for raycasting acceleration in Three.js (recommended in official docs and Discord). It provides O(log n) triangle intersection via bounding volume hierarchy, plus firstHitOnly for early-exit. Custom spatial indexing would duplicate well-tested functionality. The package adds ~35KB gzipped and is compatible with three ^0.183.

design_decisions.decision: Apply three-mesh-bvh prototype patches at module scope in consuming files
design_decisions.rationale: three-mesh-bvh works by patching BufferGeometry.prototype.computeBoundsTree and Mesh.prototype.raycast. Applying patches at module scope in meshManager.ts and selection.ts keeps them co-located with their usage. An alternative dedicated bvh.ts init module adds indirection for just two simple prototype assignments.

design_decisions.decision: Use 'in' operator for sync key membership instead of Set allocation
design_decisions.rationale: Current code creates new Set(Object.keys(meshes)) on every sync call. For Record<string, MeshData> objects, 'key in meshes' provides O(1) property lookup without any allocation. Behavioral equivalent, but avoids one Set + Array allocation per call.

design_decisions.decision: Use try/finally for global stub restoration in tests instead of afterEach
design_decisions.rationale: The resize test overrides globalThis.ResizeObserver. Restoring in a try/finally within the test body is preferred over afterEach because it keeps the save/restore co-located with the override (easier to reason about), and avoids polluting the shared afterEach with cleanup for a single test's specific stub. The finally block guarantees restoration even if assertions throw.

design_decisions.decision: On createMeshFromData BVH failure: dispose resources, log error, return null (skip mesh)
design_decisions.rationale: When computeBoundsTree() throws during mesh creation, the geometry and material have been allocated but the mesh has not yet been added to meshMap or scene. Disposing both and returning null prevents GPU resource leaks. The mesh is simply skipped — it will be retried on the next sync() call with fresh data. Logging the error (with entity path) ensures the failure is visible rather than silent. Returning null (rather than re-throwing) prevents a single bad mesh from blocking sync of all other meshes in the same batch.

design_decisions.decision: On updateMeshGeometry BVH failure: remove mesh entirely via removeMesh(), let next sync re-create it
design_decisions.rationale: When computeBoundsTree() throws during a geometry update, the mesh's render buffers have already been mutated but the BVH is stale. This mesh cannot be left in the scene because raycasts would use the wrong spatial index, producing silently incorrect hit results. Calling removeMesh() fully disposes the mesh (geometry, material, BVH) and removes it from both the scene and meshMap. On the next sync() call, the mesh will be absent from meshMap and re-created from scratch via createMeshFromData, which builds a fresh BVH. This is the rollback strategy (option b from the review) and is preferred over a BVH-invalid flag because it reuses existing cleanup infrastructure and avoids introducing a new mesh validity state that all raycast callers would need to check.

reuse.what: Existing Three.js vi.mock() infrastructure in all viewport test files
reuse.how: Extend existing MockBufferGeometry to add computeBoundsTree/disposeBoundsTree mock methods. Create MockEdgesGeometry following the same pattern as existing MockWireframeGeometry. Add addEventListener/removeEventListener to the controls mock object in Viewport.test.tsx.

OrbitControls already dispatches 'change' events when camera moves (including during damping update cycles). Viewport listens via controls.controls.addEventListener('change', requestRender) — no changes to controls.ts needed.
The controls module already returns the OrbitControls instance with addEventListener/removeEventListener via ControlsContext.controls. No interface changes needed.

reuse.what: Existing rAF-based test infrastructure in Viewport.test.tsx
reuse.how: The test file already has rafCallbacks array, rafIdCounter, and mock requestAnimationFrame/cancelAnimationFrame. Render-on-demand tests reuse this pattern to capture and selectively fire frame callbacks to verify rendering behavior.

reuse.what: Existing wireframe test patterns in selection.test.ts
reuse.how: Tests for setSelected already verify geometry creation, scene.add, dispose, and material cleanup. EdgesGeometry tests follow the same pattern, just with the updated mock class name.

reuse.what: Existing removeMesh() function for BVH update failure rollback
reuse.how: removeMesh() already handles full cleanup: disposeBoundsTree, geometry.dispose, material.dispose, scene.remove, meshMap.delete. Called from updateMeshGeometry's catch block to roll back a mesh with stale BVH, reusing all existing disposal logic.

reuse.what: MockBufferGeometry.computeBoundsTree vi.fn() mock for throw simulation
reuse.how: computeBoundsTree is already a vi.fn() on MockBufferGeometry. Use mockImplementationOnce(() => { throw new Error('BVH build failed'); }) to simulate computeBoundsTree failure in specific test cases without affecting other tests.
